title: Alcea baldshuanica
taxonomy: Alcea baldshuanica is classified as a species within the genus Alcea, which is a group of flowering plants in the mallow family (Malvaceae). This genus is further classified under the order Malvales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Alcea baldshuanica is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Malvales, Family: Malvaceae, Genus: Alcea, Species: A. baldshuanica. Alcea baldshuanica's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. The exact classification of Alcea baldshuanica may also be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy.
uses: Alcea baldshuanica, also known as the Chinese hollyhock or Chinese rosy-perowan, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and stems are rich in saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is also thought to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, making it a potential treatment for various diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal herb.
benefits: Alcea baldshuanica is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Research suggests that Alcea baldshuanica may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases. Additionally, the plant's compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Further studies are needed to fully understand the health benefits of Alcea baldshuanica and its potential applications in traditional medicine.

constituents: Alcea baldshuanica, also known as the Chinese hollyhock, contains various active constituents that contribute to its potential health benefits. These constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties. preparations: Alcea baldshuanica, also known as the silk tree poppy, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. intro: Alcea baldshuanica, also known as the Bokhara mallow, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Alcea baldshuanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alcea baldshuanica is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
